title: ‘All American’ Final Season to Premiere on The CW in July
description:
Set… hike!

	The eighth and final season of the football drama “All American” will premiere on The CW on July 13.

	It will be preceded by an hour-l
body:
Set… hike!

	The eighth and final season of the football drama “All American” will premiere on The CW on July 13.

	It will be preceded by an hour-long special, “All American: The Final Season Special,” which will debut on June 22.

	Season 8 of “All American” follows Jordan, Layla, Coop, Cassius, KJ, Khalil, Amina and Preach as their individual pursuit of dreams threatens to tear apart friendships and family. The season picks up six months after the Season 7 cliffhanger and will span just one week in the Beverly and Crenshaw crew’s life: “A week that will have them wondering if everything they’ve been through over the course of their lives has prepared them for this moment. A week that will ultimately reunite old with the new, friend with enemy, and alter the course of each of their futures forever,” per the logline.
	

	The series stars Michael Evans Behling, Greta Onieogou, Bre-Z, Osy Ikhile, Alexis Chikaeze, Nathaniel Logan McIntyre, Antonio J. Bell, Kareem Grimes and Lauryn Hardy.


	“How do you say goodbye to family? Because that is what these characters, these actors, this ‘All American’ crew and staff are after eight beautiful, hard fought, seasons together. Family,” said showrunner Nkechi Okoro Carroll said in a statement. “This was the question on repeat for all of us as we crafted this final season with long hours, lots of love, and even more tears. And what we realized was that just like when your kids grow up and finally leave home, this wasn’t goodbye… It was a tear-filled I’ll see you soon. So, to our fans who have rocked with us faithfully for over a hundred episodes, we hope you enjoy this final season as much as we enjoyed making it. Thanks for taking this ride with us. We are so grateful for you… And we’ll see you soon.” 
	

	Carroll executive produces with Greg Berlanti, Jameal Turner, Sarah Schechter and David Strauss. The show is produced by Warner Bros. Television and CBS Studios in association with Berlanti Productions.

schema.headline: ‘All American’ Final Season to Premiere on The CW in July

title: ‘Never After Dark,’ ‘Obsession’ and More Among 2026 Overlook Film Festival Award Winners
description: The Overlook Film Festival, which specializes in the horror genre, has announced its audience and jury prize winners for its 10th annual edition.Hel
body: The Overlook Film Festival, which specializes in the horror genre, has announced its audience and jury prize winners for its 10th annual edition.Held April 9-12, the 2026 festival welcomed 96 filmmaker guests to New Orleans and hosted 25 sold-out screenings and events with over 8,000 attendees.The feature film Audience Award, voted on by festival attendees, went to the Opening Night film, Focus Feature’s “Obsession,” director Curry Barker’s “tale of wish-fulfillment gone wrong.” The short film Audience Award was won by Hannah Alline’s “Scissors,” which told the story of a “creep who meets his match in the form of a group of queer friends on a weekend getaway.”The festival also introduced a new section, Side Shows, which featured upcoming films that, despite not falling into the strict definition of horror, felt a good fit for the festival. Kenji Tanigaki’s “The Furious,” which features a crime-fighting father looking to save his daughter, won the inaugural Audience Award in the category.The Grand Jury Prize for Feature Film was awarded to “Never After Dark” from director Dave Boyle, while the Scariest Feature Award went to Taratoa Stappard’s “Mārama.” The jury also awarded a Special Jury Mention to the performance of Midori Francis in “Saccharine.”The features jury was composed of filmmaker Vera Drew, film programer Jongsuk Thomas Nam and journalist Jen Yamato.In the shorts prizes, the Grand Jury Prize for Short Film was awarded to the sci-fi horror film “Man Eating Pussy” by Lee Lawson; Scariest Short went to “Ghoststory” from director Alex Jacobs, with an honorable mention for Best Kitty being awarded to “House Cat” star Katy Wicker; and the Doomscroll Award for Most Harrowing Digital Nightmare went to “Homemade Gatorade” by Carter Amelia Davis.The 2026 Overlook Film Festival Short Film Jury was composed of journalist and playwright Sharai Bohannon, author and film critic Payton McCarty-Simas and filmmaker Jenn Wexler.The 2026 festival also featured a special appearance from makeup artist Rick Baker, who presented a special 45th anniversary screening of “An American Werewolf in London” and received the festival’s Master of Horror Award.Also present to unveil their new film “Family Movie” were Kevin Bacon, Kyra Sedgwick and Travis Bacon. The new horror-comedy was made as a family, with Kevin Bacon serving as director and actor, Kyra Sedgwick acting and Travis Bacon acting and providing music for the film.Other highlights also included the Centerpiece screening of Neon’s “Leviticus” from director Adrian Chiarella; the Closing Night screening of Damian McCarthy’s “Hokum”; a 100th anniversary screening of “A Page of Madness,” the first-ever Japanese horror film; and the Boulet Brothers kicking oﬀ their new tour with “The Boulet Brothers’ Dragula: Titans Tour,” amongst others. The festival is programmed by co-founders Landon Zakheim & Michael Lerman; programmer Daniel Crooke; short film programmer Katie Rife; and programming manager Cameron Asharian.

schema.headline: ‘Never After Dark,’ ‘Obsession’ and More Among 2026 Overlook Film Festival Award Winners

title: Hulu to Live Stream ‘Get Real House’ Event Featuring Reality TV Royalty (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Hulu is bringing back its Get Real House for the second year in a row — and this time, it’s not just a party for those in attendance in Los Angeles.
body:
Hulu is bringing back its Get Real House for the second year in a row — and this time, it’s not just a party for those in attendance in Los Angeles. The event, which features announcements, discussions, and of course, a party, will live stream on Hulu on Wednesday, April 22, at 4:30 p.m. PT. “Access Hollywood” and “House Guest” host Scott Evans will emcee the event.

	“Hulu’s Get Real House will give fans front-row access to major announcements, exclusive first looks and surprise talent moments as they happen,” the press release reads. The event will be available to rewatch on Hulu afterward.
	

	The event brings together the most talked-about names in Disney’s unscripted slate. Last year’s inaugural event included talent from “American Idol,” “Bachelor in Paradise,” “The Golden Bachelor,” “Dancing with the Stars,” “Jimmy Kimmel Live!,” “The Kardashians,” “Love Thy Nader,” “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” and “Vanderpump Villa.”


	Last year, “Dancing With the Stars” pros Rylee Arnold and Ezra Sosak, hosts Alfonso Ribeiro and Julianne Hough, and judge Derek Hough announced the show’s first celebrity contestant of the season, Robert Irwin, who ended up winning Season 34. Meanwhile, the Mel Owens was announced as “The Golden Bachelor” while “Bachelor in Paradise” revealed a new filming location and the addition of Hannah Brown.

	Variety will be covering announcements coming out of the 2026 event.

	Hulu’s “Get Real House Live” is produced by Embassy Row, a Sony Pictures Television company, in association with Walt Disney Television Alternative. Michael Davies and Lauren Mandel executive produce.
	

	Watch the trailer for the Get Real House live below:

schema.headline: Hulu to Live Stream ‘Get Real House’ Event Featuring Reality TV Royalty (EXCLUSIVE)

title: Emmys: Lead Actress (Limited/Movie) — Carey Mulligan Takes Early Lead With ‘Beef’ as Kerry Washington Gains Buzz for ‘Imperfect Women’
description:
Apr 16, 2026 9:35am PT	
	

	
	Emmys: Lead Actress (Limited/Movie) — Carey Mulligan Takes Early Lead With ‘Beef’ as Kerry Washington Gains Buzz
body:
Apr 16, 2026 9:35am PT	
	

	
	Emmys: Lead Actress (Limited/Movie) — Carey Mulligan Takes Early Lead With ‘Beef’ as Kerry Washington Gains Buzz for ‘Imperfect Women’
	



			
			
	
						

									Netflix
	
					


							
		

	Variety Awards Circuit section is the home for all awards news and related content throughout the year, featuring the following: the official predictions for the upcoming Oscars, Emmys, Grammys and Tony Awards ceremonies, curated by Variety chief awards editor Clayton Davis. The prediction pages reflect the current standings in the race and do not reflect personal preferences for any individual contender. As other formal (and informal) polls suggest, competitions are fluid and subject to change based on buzz and events. Predictions are updated every Thursday.



	

					Kerry Washington, “Imperfect Women” 
									Apple TV
	




	Outstanding Lead Actress in a Limited or Anthology Series or Movie (Updated: April 16, 2026): The lead actress (limited) race does not appear as deep as in years past, which could open the door for a few surprises.

	Netflix dropped the second season of its anthology series “Beef” this week, with three-time Oscar nominee Carey Mulligan delivering standout work that could pave the way for her first career Emmy nomination. 

	The streaming giant has a strong slate of contenders this cycle, including Emmy winner Claire Danes in the fall miniseries “The Beast in Me,” Tessa Thompson in “His and Hers,” which became one of the most-watched titles in Netflix history, and two-time Oscar winner Sally Field in the upcoming film adaptation of Shelby Van Pelt’s novel “Remarkably Bright Creatures.” Notably, performers from limited series and television movies compete together in these acting categories.

	Sarah Pidgeon’s portrayal of Carolyn Bessette in Ryan Murphy’s “Love Story” is another performance gaining traction, positioning her as a potential first-time nominee — and possibly even a winner.A wave of fall contenders could also reemerge in the conversation, including Sarah Snook in Peacock’s “All Her Fault” and Patricia Arquette in Hulu’s “Murdaugh: Death in the Family,” both of whom may benefit from renewed visibility.

	Also in the mix is the one-two punch from Apple TV+’s “Imperfect Women,” the psychological thriller miniseries led by Elisabeth Moss and Kerry Washington. With 15 career Emmy nominations across “Mad Men,” “Top of the Lake” and “The Handmaid’s Tale,” Moss remains a familiar and formidable presence voters may readily embrace. However, much of the buzz has centered on Washington’s performance. With nine career nominations spanning acting and producing, she could break through — though she may need broader support for the series in other categories to secure a spot.

	All official category submissions have yet to be confirmed, and the field could shift before the end of the eligibility cycle.

	The Emmy Awards timeline begins with nomination-round voting from June 11-22, with nominations announced on July 8. The final voting runs from Aug. 17-26, leading into the Creative Arts Awards and Governors Gala on Sept. 5–6, culminating with the Emmy Awards ceremony on Sept. 14 on NBC.


			
			
			
										
					Image Credit: FX

schema.headline: Emmys: Lead Actress (Limited/Movie) — Carey Mulligan Takes Early Lead With ‘Beef’ as Kerry Washington Gains Buzz for ‘Imperfect Women’

title: LIV Golf Mexico City 2026: Where to Watch the Golf Tournament Live Online
description:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	With the
body:
If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

	With the first tournament in North America of the season, the 2026 LIV Golf Mexico City (formerly LIV Golf Mayakoba) tees off starting on Thursday, Apr. 16 and goes until Sunday, Apr. 19 at Club De Golf Chapultepec in Mexico.

	In its fourth annual of play in Mexico City, the pro golf season extends tournaments from 54 holes to 72 holes in a new format change.


	Going into Mexico City, Spanish golfer Jon Rahm still sits high at the top of the leaderboard with 579.00 points after five tournaments. However, American Bryson DeChambeau climbs up the ranks in second place, despite winning South Africa. Elvis Smylie, Anthony Kim and Thomas Detry round out the top five.


	As for team play, Ripper GC, led by Australian Cameron Smith, is at the top of the pack with 85.75 after five tournaments.

	During the tournament, 57 players are spread over 13 four-player teams with five wild card positions, which are up for grabs. Players get compete in teams and as individuals to gain points throughout the season. 

	Meanwhile, LIV Golf Mexico City features top pro players, including Joaquin Niemann, Talor Gooch, Patrick Reed, Bubba Watson, Dustin Johnson and others.
	
		When Does 2026 LIV Golf Mexico City Start?	

	Starting on Thursday, Apr. 16, with tee time at 3:15 p.m. ET/12:15 p.m. PT, the LIV Golf Mexico City tournament airs on FOX and FOX Sports (FS1). Check out a complete schedule here.

	You don’t have to miss any of the action, even without cable. You can access the game on your TV with one of these affordable indoor digital HD TV antennas. Meanwhile, the LIV Golf Mexico City also livestreams online on a wide range of streaming platforms, such as DirecTV, Fubo, Hulu + Live TV and others.


	In fact, some services offer free trials that you can use to livestream the pro golf tournament online for free.

	Want to watch online? Check out the best ways to stream LIV Golf Mexico City, below:

		

						
			
	

					DirecTV
	
					
		

					DirecTV 		

	
		DirecTV is one of the best ways for cable-cutters to access live sports and TV from home.	
		
	

	Starting at $59.99 for the first month of service ($94.99/month), you get access to the streaming service’s “Choice” package for LIV Golf Mexico City on FOX and FS1. It also includes NBC, ABC, CBS, ESPN and other networks — more than 125 channels.

		

						
			
	

					Sling TV
	
					
		

					Sling TV		

	
		Sling is one of the best options for affordable live streaming.	
		
	

	The Sling Blue package, which includes FOX and FS1, goes for $54.99/month, while sports fans can add the Sports Extra package for FS2 to their plan for an additional $11/month. Please note: Pricing and channel availability are subject to your local TV market.

		

						
			
	

					Fubo
	
					
		

					Fubo		

	
		The streamer offers a 5-day free trial to try out the service before you commit.	
		
	

	Fubo is one of the best options for streaming the pro golf tournament. The service features LIV Golf Mexico City on FOX and FS1. The streamer even has more than 200 live TV channels for cable-free streaming with prices starting at $48.99 for the first month ($73.99/month afterwards).

		

						
			
	

					Hulu
	
					
		

					Hulu + Live TV		

	
		Prices start at $89.99/month.	
		
	

	For another great option for the golf tournament, check out Hulu + Live TV for live channel streaming LIV Golf Mexico City with access to FOX and FS1. It has more than 95 other channels, as well as access to hit originals on Disney+, Hulu and ESPN Unlimited.

schema.headline: LIV Golf Mexico City 2026: Where to Watch the Golf Tournament Live Online

title: Ariana Madix, Warner Records’ Dalia Ganz, LA28 CMO Alex Merchan, Qualcomm CMO Don McGuire and Patrick Ta Join Variety’s Entertainment Marketing Summit
description: Variety has announced additional speakers for the 2026 Entertainment Marketing Summit, presented by Deloitte, on April 22 in Los Angeles. Speakers w
body:
Variety has announced additional speakers for the 2026 Entertainment Marketing Summit, presented by Deloitte, on April 22 in Los Angeles. Speakers will include Ariana Madix, actress, “Love Island USA” host and co-founder of Something About Her; Warner Records’ EVP of Marketing & Artist Development Dalia Ganz; LA28 CMO Alex Merchan; Qualcomm CMO Don McGuire and Patrick Ta, celebrity makeup artist and founder of Patrick Ta Beauty.
	

	Madix and Ta will break down how they’ve built a brand from the ground up, scaled their platforms, and activated their existing followings to fuel new creative and business opportunities.


	Ganz, EVP of Marketing and Artist Development at Warner Records, Carter Gregory, content creator and music executive and media personality, Todd Lichten, Head of Entertainment and Lifestyle Partnerships, Roblox, and Brent Mitchell, VP of Social Media and Influencer Marketing, Sephora, will join the conversation on how to leverage creators and social platforms as marketing tools for entertainment and consumer brands for success in today’s creator economy.


	Merchan, CMO at LA28, will speak with Michelle McGuire, Chief Data & AI Officer at Deloitte US, about how technology is shaping the way LA28 Olympic and Paralympic Games are delivered to fans locally and worldwide.

	McGuire, CMO at Qualcomm, joins Jasmine Jacks, Principal of Deloitte Consulting LLP, to speak about how AI accelerates brands to move at the speed of culture–showing up in the right moments with consistency and intent.

	In other newly added highlights, Jennifer Maas, Senior TV Business and Games reporter at Variety, will moderate the “Where Fandoms Collide & Culture Is Rewritten” conversation with Havas Play’s Andrea Isaac, Managing Partner, North America, and Juliet Tierney, SVP of Partnerships and Culture Strategy, North America. They will unpack what it takes to move from one-off activations to connected cultural ecosystems, and how to design for participation across different fandoms.
	

	Marianna Hewitt, co-founder of Summer Fridays, and Nic Paul, co-founder and president of Spotter, will join the previously announced “In the Director’s Chair: Brand Storytelling Masters” panel discussing how the growth of media platforms helps brands creatively break through across varied demographics. The panel also includes Margaret Walker, EVP, entertainment marketing, NBCUniversal, Nicole Parlapiano, CMO, Tubi, Claudine Cheever, CMO, Pinterest, and Jasmine Taylor, VP, brand marketing, Instacart.

	Innovative Artist Creators’ Jack Harris will share insights on the current state and future of the creator economy, as well as how he is leading Innovative’s creator representation business with a model that sets a new standard in the space.

	The speakers join previously announced programming including keynote conversations featuring Pam Abdy, Warner Bros. Motion Picture Group Co-Chair and CEO, and Tina Knowles, businesswoman, fashion designer, art collector, philanthropist and activist.

	Deloitte is the presenting partner of the summit. Please see Deloitte’s website for a detailed description of its legal structure. NBCUniversal is a Premier Partner of the event, with official partners Havas Play and Innovative Artists, and supporting partners Discord, NVE Experience Agency, Spotter, StackAdapt, & Vizio. Get your ticket today at variety.com/entmar.

schema.headline: Ariana Madix, Warner Records’ Dalia Ganz, LA28 CMO Alex Merchan, Qualcomm CMO Don McGuire and Patrick Ta Join Variety’s Entertainment Marketing Summit

title: ‘Outlander: Blood of My Blood’ Unveils Season 2 Teaser Ahead of ‘Outlander’ Series Finale
description:
Starz has unveiled a first-look teaser and images from Season 2 of “Outlander: Blood of My Blood.”

	The prequel series, which will return this fall
body:
Starz has unveiled a first-look teaser and images from Season 2 of “Outlander: Blood of My Blood.”

	The prequel series, which will return this fall on Starz, continues to expand the time-traveling universe by focusing on the love stories of Jamie Fraser and Claire Beauchamp’s parents. Season 1 introduced two parallel romances: Julia (Hermione Corfield) and Henry Beauchamp (Jeremy Irvine), as well as Ellen MacKenzie (Harriet Slater) and Brian Fraser (Jamie Roy).
	

	According to the official synopsis, “As Season Two unfolds, the two young couples will be tested and separated by forces beyond their control, as every clan chooses a side in the rebellion.”


	The first season followed Henry and Julia’s relationship as it began “through the letters they exchanged from the war-torn battlefields of WWI,” before an unexpected journey through time left them stranded in 18th century Scotland. Meanwhile, Ellen and Brian’s story centered on a forbidden romance between rival clans, with the pair choosing love over “tradition, politics and familial obligations.”

	By the end of the season, both couples faced pivotal turning points: Henry and Julia arrived at the standing stones of Craigh na Dun in hopes of returning to their own time, while Ellen and Brian’s escape was cut short by the onset of the 1715 Jacobite Rising.

	The new season will pick up from that moment, with the looming conflict shaping the fate of both relationships.

	The rollout comes ahead of the “Outlander” series finale, set to air May 15. All previous seasons of “Outlander” and Season 1 of “Blood of My Blood” are currently available to stream on the Starz app and on-demand platforms.
	

	“Outlander: Blood of My Blood” is produced by Sony Pictures Television, with Matthew B. Roberts serving as showrunner.

	Watch the teaser trailer below.

schema.headline: ‘Outlander: Blood of My Blood’ Unveils Season 2 Teaser Ahead of ‘Outlander’ Series Finale

title: ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 Premiere Draws 8.5 Million Viewers in First Three Days
description: HBO is no doubt feeling euphoric. The Season 3 premiere of “Euphoria” has pulled in 8.5 million U.S. viewers across HBO and HBO Max in its first thr
body:
HBO is no doubt feeling euphoric. The Season 3 premiere of “Euphoria” has pulled in 8.5 million U.S. viewers across HBO and HBO Max in its first three days of availability. 

	Per HBO, that is a 44% increase over the Season 2 premiere, which aired in January 2022. The four-year hiatus did little to dampen enthusiasm for the series, which saw stars Zendaya, Sydney Sweeney, Jacob Elordi, and more return. 


	While the first two seasons focused on the characters in high school, the new season takes place five years after the Season 2 finale. Since then, Rue (Zendaya) has begun working as a drug mule for Laurie (Martha Kelly), Nate (Elordi) has taken over his father’s construction business. He is also preparing to marry Cassie (Sweeney), who is launching an OnlyFans account. 
	

	This season also sees the return of Maude Apatow, Alexa Demie, Hunter Schafer, Colman Domingo, Kelly, and Chloe Cherry among others. New stars this season include Adewale Akinnuoye-Agbaje, Darrell Britt-Gibson, Sharon Stone, and Marshawn Lynch. 

	The premiere episode also features tributes to late series stars Angus Cloud and Eric Dane, as well as executive producer Kevin Turen. Cloud, who played drug dealer Fezco in Seasons 1 and 2, died of a drug overdose in 2023. Dane, meanwhile, died from complications of ALS in early 2026. Turen died on Nov. 12, 2023 from a cardiac emergency while driving.

	Elsewhere on the HBO schedule, the finale of the critically-acclaimed limited series “DTF St. Louis” drew 3.5 million crossplatform viewers in its first three days. The show has seen consistent growth week-to-week, with the finale finishing 40% above the premiere. Per HBO, the show is averaging 6.5 million viewers per episode.

schema.headline: ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 Premiere Draws 8.5 Million Viewers in First Three Days

title: Netflix Expands Video Podcast Slate With Shows from ‘Rotten Mango’ Creator Stephanie Soo, Evan Ross Katz, NBC News’ Ellison Barber and Magician David Kwong
description:
Netflix is bulking up its podcast lineup, announcing five new shows coming to the streaming platform.

	The company dove into the space last year, i
body:
Netflix is bulking up its podcast lineup, announcing five new shows coming to the streaming platform.

	The company dove into the space last year, inking distribution deals with Spotify, iHeartMedia and Barstool Sports. Netflix also released original podcasts “The Pete Davidson Show” and sports analysis and talk show “The White House,” hosted by Michael Irvin.

	Now it’s adding several new shows to the queue: pop-culture interview podcast “We’re Back! With Brian Williams,” hosted by the former NBC News anchor; true-crime podcast “The Rotten Files,” from Stephanie Soo (known for her popular podcast “Rotten Mango”); “Shut Up Evan” hosted by Evan Ross Katz; “Allegedly,” a true-crime show hosted by NBC News correspondent Ellison Barber; and “The Puzzle Room with David Kwong.”
	

	Soo launched “Rotten Mango” in 2020, and it has become one of the most popular podcasts in the true-crime category. She also hosts comedy podcast “Moral of the Story,” where she tells her husband about the most unhinged real-life scandals and internet dramas from around the world. She’s repped by UTA.

	“Netflix is putting meaningful creative power in the hands of content creators, which is a really rare opportunity,” Soo said in a statement. “We’re ready to hit the ground running.”

	Katz’s “Shut Up Evan,” inspired by his GLAAD Media Award-nominated series of the same name, will premiere May 1 on Netflix, airing twice a week. “Deep in the DMs With…” will feature intimate one-on-one conversation with actors, musicians, and tastemakers shaping the culture on Tuesdays. Then, he will be joined on Fridays by a rotating panel of celebrity guests for “The Group Chat” to dish on the buzziest topics of the week. The show is produced by 32 Flavors.
	

	“It’s an honor (understatement) to bring my podcast over to the wondrous world of Netflix. As an OG since the red envelope days, there’s no better place to call home,” Katz commented. In addition to his career as an entertainment journalist, Katz has hosted companion podcasts for series including “The Comeback,” “Love Story,” “The Beauty” and “White Lotus.”

	Barber’s “Allegedly” true-crime weekly video podcast bows on Netflix April 29. In each episode she will break down a high-profile case with a “trusted friend with expertise in the world of crime, or a person central to the story,” per Netflix. The first episode will discuss new revelations in the Gabby Petito case; the second episode unpacks the recent high-profile investigation regarding real estate brokers the Alexander brothers; and in the third, Barber speaks with cult psychology expert Christine Marie from “Trust Me: The False Prophet.”

	“Allegedly” is produced by NBC News Studios. Executive producers are Liz Cole, Kimberley Ferdinando and Christopher Cassel.

	Finally, video podcast series “The Puzzle Room With David Kwong” launches on May 11, hosted by the magician, mentalist, and New York Times crossword constructor. In each episode, he creates a personalized puzzle for a pair of celebrity guests. Produced by Pod People, the series is designed for viewers to play along in a race to see who can answer first. Each episode “ends with a moment of mentalism or head-scratching illusions,” according to Netflix.

	Pictured above (l. to r.): Stephanie Soo, Evan Ross Katz, Ellison Barber, David Kwong

schema.headline: Netflix Expands Video Podcast Slate With Shows from ‘Rotten Mango’ Creator Stephanie Soo, Evan Ross Katz, NBC News’ Ellison Barber and Magician David Kwong

title: Rodney Crowell Releases Duet With Guy Clark, ‘Are You One of Us?,’ to Preview Lost Album He Rediscovered After 20 Years: ‘I Truly Had Forgotten I Had an Album in the Can’ [EXCLUSIVE]
description: It might seem odd that anyone could record an album as first-rate as “Then Again” and then not only shelve it, but forget about it. But not everyone
body:
It might seem odd that anyone could record an album as first-rate as “Then Again” and then not only shelve it, but forget about it. But not everyone is Rodney Crowell, who has been busy enough following his muse that it hadn’t really reoccurred to him that he had an excellent “lost” album sitting in his archives from 20 years ago, much less that he should finally put it out.

	After a timely rediscovery, that abandoned project will finally see daylight, as New West Records issues “Then Again” on June 26. To preview it, Variety has the premiere of the album’s lead track, “Are You One of Us?,” a vocal collaboration with a fellow legend of Americana, the late Guy Clark. Fans of either artist are likely to agree that, apart from Clark no longer being with us, the 2006-vintage track sounds like it could have been recorded yesterday, between its audio crispness and political theme.
	




	“Honestly, I’d forgotten about the recording altogether, and when I heard it, I said, ‘Here’s Guy — wow. This oughta be out there!’” Crowell tells Variety. “And that’s which I thought about just pulling it and releasing it” as a stand-alone single. Then he realized the rest of the shelved album was just as strong, “so I called my manager and I said, ‘Hey, we just had an album out [the 2025 release ‘Airline Highway’], but who cares? The world’s different now — it doesn’t matter. Nobody cares. Let’s put another one out there.'” (The album is up for pre-order on vinyl and CD here.) 


	The word “duet” may belong in quotes, since “Are You One of Us?” does not have both of them singing — Crowell handles the main duties, while Clark answers most of the lines in the verses with snarky spoken-word rejoinders. He explains how the track evolved to include his longtime Texas singer-songwriter compatriot.
	

	“You know, Guy and I were old friends,” Crowell says, which will come as an understatement to many fans. “We were the kind of friends that loved each other even though we’d had our falling out. I had Guy Clark pissed off at me, and that’s some pretty tall cotton, when Guy Clark was mad at you! We patched it up. It was on me. I was the asshole in that one. But we were still working on getting back together when we did [“Are You One of Us?”]. And I knew that for what I needed with the song, Guy had the right feeling, and Guy’s a great song actor… or was… He is. And it was like having John Gielgud come in and do the part, so I knew it was gonna be perfect and he did it in one take, and it just completely just yanks the rug under my narrator. It was a conversation, and it was so much fun. That song, I mean, it certainly would fit today — you know, the right and the left, and the twain shall never meet.” 

	Clark and Crowell are not meant to be foils to one another in the song. “Those two narrators actually are on the same team. Because when I wrote the song, the added part there is kind of a reiteration of what I’m saying, but from the point of view of a pretty snarly dude. So we aren’t at cross purposes because those two narrators are basically on the same side of, ‘Hey, we’re cool and you’re not. We’re liberal and you’re not. So, take that,'” he laughs.

	He rediscovered the 2006 album project while doing some spring cleaning… well, fall cleaning, to be accurate. As for why he set it aside 20 years ago, Crowell explains that he had completed a sort of trilogy at the time, in his mind, and this project felt like it was extending that to an unnecessary tetralogy… like he was spinning his wheels, as fresh as it now sounds.

	“My home studio had become cluttered and I had a part-time assistant helping me unclutter, and we found a hard drive and loaded it into the computer and I said, ‘Oh, I recognize that.’ Richard Dodd had originally mastered the record way back when. But I put it on the shelf because I had made ‘The Houston Kid’ and ‘Fate’s Right Hand’ and ‘The Outsider’ in the same studio, and also made a Cicadas album [with a side-project band]. It sounded like I heard all my production techniques glaring at me. So I decided to set it aside, and I spent a lot of my own money on it and went out to L.A. and made a record with Joe Henry, ‘Sex and Gasoline,’ instead.” (He re-recorded a few of the songs that had been slated for “Then Again” for “Sex and Gasoline”; for the final track list of the new release, he only included one song that overlaps between the two records, “40 Winters,” saying, “I like both versions.”)  
	

	“It felt too familiar at the time. And I worked with a pretty tight knit group of musicians — Michael Rose, Steuart Smith, and these musicians — and their eyes were bugging out and they were going, ‘You’re not putting it out?’ They all loved it. And I said, ‘Well, I love you, but I’m gonna go out to California and do something completely different.’ I needed to learn something new about myself. And I went out and worked with Joe Henry in his home studio, and I just completely put myself in his hands. I didn’t stay for mixing or anything; I went and performed the songs live, there was no overdubbing, I sang every note, and then I got the record [from Henry]. He sent me a disc of the mixes. I listened to it in my car and went, ‘This is great.’ So I succeeded in doing what I wanted to do, which was make some music that didn’t sound like anything I had made. 

	“But these 20 years later, I put [‘Now Then’] on and it sounded good to me. I didn’t hear my production quote-unquote ‘techniques’ the way I would have then.  It was just a fresh batch of songs.”



	

					Rodney Crowell
									Claudia Church
	



	To round out the album, since he removed some of the songs that also appeared on “Sex and Gasoline,” Crowell added a song he’d recorded but never released about 10 years ago, and another one he put out last fall. “I added ‘If I Could Speak to Leonard,’ which was something I recorded before Leonard Cohen died. I always wanted him to hear it, but I didn’t get around to getting it out. And then I added the one song as a bonus track at the end, ‘Go Light a Candle,’ and there you go. You have an album.”

	“Go Light a Candle” features Emmylou Harris and Lera Lynn as featured vocalists. He explains of the track, which he put out digitally in 2025: “It could have been a response to yesterday,” he says, referring to Iraq conflicts in the news. “I mean, same response today. I wrote that after the last election and around the time the gentleman overtook the White House. I was talking about, ‘God, I’ve written the angry political songs before.’ And a friend of mine, Sam Baker, said, ‘Ah, man, go light a candle.’ I went, ‘There you go.’”




	He’s excited to have two albums out for people to hear in less than 12 months, as a champion of the artform.
	

	“I like making albums. You know, I don’t think they’re that fashionable anymore, but it’s sure fun to make them. To me, it’s like an art show. It’s like, well, I’m gonna hang 10 paintings up here. That’s my mindset when I do it. I don’t wanna let go of that. I enjoy that. And just because culture has changed doesn’t mean it’s not fun to have an art show with 10 or 11 songs.”

	He adds, “I’m productive, so you know what, I’m just gonna start sticking it out there, man. Who cares? It’s what I love to do, and people can partake or not. One of the things about the modern age and music  is that you can’t really let your ego get involved, because you’ll get your feelings hurt pretty quickly, because you can just as easily be ignored as you can be recognized and appreciated for what you do. So my mindset on it is, I’ll just do it because I love to do it, and it’s a great job to have, and I don’t take it for granted.

	“To the extent that  anybody listens to the music you record, I’m just grateful, because recorded music is not as precious as it used to be. Back in the day, when we read all the liner notes and all of it, records were precious. [Elton John’s] ‘Tumbleweed Connection’ is still precious to me. And so I’m still shooting for that, and when I rediscovered this, I went, ‘Well, this is good. I can be proud of this.'”



	

					Rodney Crowell ‘Then Again’ album cover
									Lynn Goldsmith
	



	“Then Again” track listing:


I Won’t Lie
Are You One of Us? (feat. Guy Clark)
If I Could Speak To Leonard
Bring It on Home To Memphis
The Ballad of Artemis and Orion
Sing Your Heart Out (feat. Kieran Goss & Annie Kinsella)
Whatcha Gonna Do Now #2 (feat. Lyle Lovett & Chely Wright)
The Has-Been Vents His Spleen
40 Winters
Go Light a Candle (feat. Emmylou Harris & Lera Lynn)

schema.headline: Rodney Crowell Releases Duet With Guy Clark, ‘Are You One of Us?,’ to Preview Lost Album He Rediscovered After 20 Years: ‘I Truly Had Forgotten I Had an Album in the Can’ [EXCLUSIVE]

title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. Reacts When Quizzed Over Chopped Raccoon Penis, on Video
description:
Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
                          Mum On Being the Bobbitt of Roadkill!!! 🦝
body:
Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
                          Mum On Being the Bobbitt of Roadkill!!! 🦝
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              April 16, 2026
                            8:17 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  April 16, 2026
                                9:24 AM PDT
                                    
              
    
      
        
                    
                  
      
                    TMZ.com
          
    
          RFK Jr.’s keeping a tight, trash-can lid on his dead raccoon penis saga -- ’cause when TMZ DC went digging, his reaction was straight dumpster gold!
      
    
          Check the clip -- TMZ put Trump’s Health and Human Services chief on the spot Thursday with the question no one else would touch ... what happened to that raccoon penis, and where’s it at now? RFK Jr.’s answer? Radio silence ... followed by a laugh doing all the talking.
      
    
          You gotta see the video -- 'cause that wasn’t just any laugh ... it was a tail-end-of-the-truth kinda laugh ... like there’s a whole extra layer to this chopped-up critter story he’s keeping buried.
      

                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          In his new biography "RFK Jr.: The Fall and Rise," he admits when he stumbled on the roadkill ... he took matters into his own hands -- literally -- chopping off the animal’s bits for further study.
      
    
          Clearly, RFK Jr. ain’t about to let anyone paw through the full story!

schema.headline: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. Reacts When Quizzed Over Chopped Raccoon Penis, on Video

title: 'Baywatch' Star David Charvet Accused of Hit-and-Run After Striking And Killing Dog
description:
David Charvet
                          Accused of Hit-and-Run After His Truck Kills Dog in Malib
body:
David Charvet
                          Accused of Hit-and-Run After His Truck Kills Dog in Malibu
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              April 16, 2026
                            7:46 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  April 16, 2026
                                9:24 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          update
  
    
          9:07 AM PT -- A law enforcement source tells TMZ ... they do not consider the incident a hit-and-run since David called authorities after it happened -- even if he was not directly on scene. A deputy is with Vera and her family right now, taking a "traffic collision" report.
      
    
          Info being gathered includes whether the dog died -- and whether it was leashed at the time. Vera told us yes, the dog died, and yes, her three dogs were on a leash.
      
    
          However, David claims the dog was NOT on a leash at the time of the collision -- and he tried to swerve to avoid hitting the dog.
      
    
          8:07 AM PT -- the Lost Hills sheriff station tells TMZ that they've been in contact with Vera ... and they're sending deputies to her home to take a report.
      
    
          "Baywatch" OG star David Charvet struck and killed a dog Thursday morning ... TMZ has learned.
      
    
          Vera Errico tells us, just before 6 AM, she was walking her 3 dogs -- all on leashes -- in a gated Malibu community, when Charvet came along in his pickup truck.
      

                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          Vera says Charvet didn't stop after hitting the dog ... rather, she says he drove outside the gated community and she believes he then called the police -- something a driver is required to do if involved in an accident that results in injury or property damage.
      
    
          She says his truck struck Sunday, her 11-year-old white English Labrador. She and her husband rushed Sunday to the vet, where the animal was euthanized.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          Vera and her husband are on the way to the L.A. County Sheriff's Office to file a police report -- for hit-and-run.
      
    
          We spoke with Charvet, who gave us a "no comment" and told us to reach out to his publicist. We did so ... so far, no word back.

title: Brian Hooker's 'Soulmate' Yacht Docked in Bahamas as He Leaves to See Ailing Mom
description:
Brian Hooker
                          See Ya, Bahamas ... Gotta Go See My Sick Mom
body:
Brian Hooker
                          See Ya, Bahamas ... Gotta Go See My Sick Mom
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              April 16, 2026
                            7:14 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          Brian Hooker hightailed it out of the Bahamas to visit his ailing mother, leaving behind the yacht named "Soulmate." His wife Lynette's location is still a mystery nearly two weeks after she allegedly fell overboard on a dinghy as it was traveling to the larger boat.
      
    
          Brian's attorney, Terrel Butler, tells TMZ ... Brian left the Bahamas Wednesday to attend to his mother, who is sick.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          While he's gone, Brian's keeping his boat anchored in Marsh Harbour, a town in the Abaco Islands, Bahamas, while police and the U.S. Coast Guard continue their search for Lynette. TMZ just obtained photos of Brian's "Soulmate" yacht parked at a dock.
      
    
          A Coast Guard official told TMZ Tuesday ... they are still "conducting a criminal investigation into the disappearance of Mrs. Hooker." And the Royal Bahamas Police Force says Brian remains a suspect in the case.
      

          As you know, Brian was released from police custody on Monday after prosecutors failed to charge him with a crime following Lynette's disappearance at sea on April 4.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
          Brian says the couple left Hope Town on an 8-foot dinghy to motor to Elbow Cay, where they were going to pick up the "Soulmate" yacht. But, Brian says, they hit rough weather during the trip, and Lynette went overboard ... she was wearing a lanyard with the boat's keys, causing it to stop. Brian paddled the boat for hours back to land and reported her missing.
      
    
          Despite numerous searches, Lynette has not been seen since the day she vanished. On Wednesday, police used a K-9 team to try to find Lynette in Hope Town, but came up empty-handed.

title: Sebastian Stan's GF Annabelle Wallis Debuts Baby Bump in NYC
description:
Sebastian Stan's GF Annabelle Wallis
                          Lovin' My New Baby Bump!!!
body:
Sebastian Stan's GF Annabelle Wallis
                          Lovin' My New Baby Bump!!!
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              April 16, 2026
                            9:07 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
          Annabelle Wallis and Sebastian Stan are expecting their first child together ... and the actress just debuted her new baby bump in NYC!
      
    
          Check out the pic ... the "Peaky Blinders" star wore a striped denim button-down, slacks and white sneakers while taking her 2 dogs on a walk through the city Monday.
      
    
          As you know ... Sebastian and Annabelle have been together for about four years, and TMZ broke the story about the pregnancy last month.
      

          The timing came during a particularly busy stretch for both stars ... Sebastian has a packed year ahead with the upcoming release of his film "Fjord" ... along with the highly anticipated Marvel installment "Avengers: Doomsday."
      
    
          And Annabelle stars alongside Jason Statham in the action film "Mutiny" -- slated to hit theaters in August -- as well as Netflix's "Unabomb," a project about the Unabomber, Ted Kaczynski.
